name: Droomen Knoll
description: A ruined fort to the east of Lankshorn, along the Ditchway. The site has multiple towers, only one of which is still standing. Much of the structure is rubble, with an open archway and crumbling walls. The rear wall has breaches choked by overgrowth, accessible to smaller folk. Strange pipe music drifts up from beneath the ruins.
notes: Four crookhorns were killed here in Session 1: Berk, Drogba, and two unnamed. A fifth — the rabid crookhorn — fled down the stairs into the dungeon.

After returning on the second day (Hayme, 24th of Simswald), the party found fresh puncture wounds in the breggle corpses from the day before — now confirmed to have been made by stirges living beneath the ruins.
The pipe music source has been located: it was Thinwhistle, a frost elf imprisoned in the cells beneath the ruins. He has since been freed.
A staircase descends from the courtyard, turning 90 degrees into the dark. The corridor below has worn flagstones and damp stone walls.

First room: a two-headed, chicken-like mutant creature was found tied by a rope to the far wall. Now dead; Gwomotom harvested the corpse. Faded frescoes line the walls — white humanoid silhouettes in an icy forest, identified by Lucien as frost elves associated with the Cold Prince.
A hallway branches from the first room and leads to a four-way intersection.

The storage room lies beyond a door in the first room. It contained a ring of candles, crookhorn bedding, and crates. The rabid crookhorn and a one-horned crookhorn were killed here. The candles produce thick orange smoke.
Mabble was found unconscious in the storage room; a trapped chest nearby contained coin, two gold-and-amber bracelets, and a Gaff letter.
A secret door in the storage room, hidden behind a high stack of crates, triggers a section of wall to swing open onto a snow-filled hallway.

The snow hallway leads to a chamber containing a raised stone pool, frozen solid. Stag skull carvings decorate the basin; glittering lights shimmer beneath the ice. Snow falls constantly from the ceiling. The walls bear long-faded frescoes. The chalice of Mallowheart was recovered from the pool in Session 3 using Brennan's blood to melt the ice. A sip of metallic silvery liquid remained in the chalice; the pool resealed itself immediately after.

Left passage from the four-way intersection: a stone sundial coated in frost. An inscription reads (in magically translated text): "On the slow creep of aeons, Mallow-heart's mercy may shine." A bas-relief behind it depicts Prince Mallowheart — a bitter-faced elf in heavy furs, crowned with thorns set with gems. Shining a lantern at the dial at seven minutes past noon melts the frost and triggers a mechanism that unlocked the cells.

From the four-way intersection, another passage leads to a sunlit room visible through the rubble from above. Five stirges were killed here by the party. A body was found in the rubble: a human man in a black cloak bearing Drune markings, drained by stirges. His effects included a golden owl torque, an arcane scroll, a platinum ring set with a topaz eye, and an orange crystal. Touching the crystal gave Lucien a vision of the Whything Stones and a voice warning of ill omens.

Feasting hall: accessible through a smashed door at the end of the sunlit room. Six large murals in recesses depict members of the frost elf court, their names magically translated: Prince Seven-Past-Noon (Mallowheart), Sir Dawn-Neverblooms, Lady Silver-So-Mirrored (with harp), Lord Slumberbane, Whisper-Of-Candlelight, and Famine-Whistles-Whither (with silver flute and three blood-red hounds added later). A heavy stone table carved with boar hunting scenes and a tower stands in the center; frost spread beneath it when it was righted.

Two cells: stone walls with iron bars. One empty, one formerly occupied by Thinwhistle (Famine-Whistles-Whither). Graffiti in the empty cell reads: "Mallowheart be cursed; hound baker Thinwhistle be doubly cursed." Both cells are now empty.
